code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test14-OCT-2015Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function is mildly impaired (elevated creatinine), with significant proteinuria and ketonuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting possible renal pathology or dehydration. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) may indicate urinary tract issues, though no infection markers are present. Glucose and electrolytes are within normal limits, but HbA1c is unexpectedly low, possibly due to anemia or recent blood loss. Recommend further renal evaluation and assessment for underlying causes of proteinuria and ketonuria.

coding.display: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
